16*Unit: 1 ➜ The Natural World*
Environmental Impacts of Logging (1-6), {p} Modern industry has caused damage to our natural 3*environment* in many ways. The air and water are filled with 3*pollutants*. One result of this is acid rain, which has caused 3*extensive* damage to vegetation in many areas. When large amounts of vegetation die off, the environment loses 3*stability*. If there are no plants to hold the soil, it starts to 3*erode*. This leads to myriad problems, including water pollution and habitat loss. 3*Defenders* of wildlife work hard to prevent further damage to natural areas.

column1:
16*Unit: 1 ➜ The Natural World*
Bird Migration (8-12), {p} Birds are 10*fascinating* to many people, and bird watching is a popular hobby. The best time to watch birds is in the early morning, because birds are usually very active at that time of day. The 10*observer* must keep still and quiet in order not to frighten the birds away. If you live in a part of the world where 10*migratory* birds spend their breeding season, then you will have the opportunity to see nest-building activity. Over the ages, different species of birds have 10*evolved* with different types of nest-building skills. It makes an interesting study to look at the different types of nests built by birds and to watch them as they build their nests. After the breeding season is over and the babies have left the nest, it is time for the birds to head for warmer parts of the world to spend the winter months. Birds 10*navigate* to their winter feeding grounds, using the stars or the sun as their guide.

16*Unit: 1 ➜ The Natural World*
Plant Life In the Taklimakan Desert (14-19), {p} Desert plants have a variety of 1*adaptations* that allow them to endure the desert environment. Because a desert is 1*extremely* dry, plants need to be able to take in as much water as possible when it rains and to store the water for a long time. Special root systems and types of leaves enable them to do this. Another source of 1*stress* in a desert is the high temperature, so desert plants need to have 1*resilience*. 1*Violent* storms can occur in a desert, and plants with strong roots will be able to endure the storms. Considering the difficult conditions in a desert, the 1*diversity* of plants that can be found there is truly amazing.

16*Unit: 2 ➜ Leisure Time*
Peripheral Vision in Sports (21-26), {p} In order to 6*perform* well, an athlete must have a number of different abilities. Naturally, she should 6*demonstrate* excellent physical skills. In addition to strength, 6*coordination* of all parts of the body while moving around the court or field is very important. The athlete also needs to have good 6*visual* abilities. She needs to be able to see what is happening around her so that she can respond to the other players’ maneuvers. She has to be 6*tolerant* of activity around her without losing her ability to focus on her own part in the game. Finally, she needs to be a fast thinker. 6*Complications* can occur in any game, and the athlete needs to be able to respond to them quickly.

He later added other kinds of performances to the show, such as clowns and 4*trained* animals. The show became very 4*popular* and the idea spread to other countries. 16*Unit: 2 ➜ Leisure Time*
Uses of Leisure Time (35-40), {p} It is crucial to acknowledge the importance of leisure-time activities. They are not merely a way to use up free time. They are important for our physical and 11*emotional* health. We need to choose activities that rest our minds and bodies so that we can feel rejuvenated when we return to work and can do our jobs more 11*industriously*. Some people enjoy 11*intellectual* pastimes — other people choose different sorts of leisure-time activities. The key is to be 11*deliberate* about choosing a pastime that is active rather than passive. Many people feel 11*reluctant* to be physically active after a tiring week at work. However, 11*authorities* tell us that this is actually the best way to decrease stress and relax.

16*Unit: 3 ➜ Transportation*
Major Subways of Europe (49-54), {p} The planning and construction of a subway system requires a great deal of time and effort. In addition to planning the routes, digging the tunnels, and laying the tracks, the stations have to be built. 4*Architects* are hired to plan the stations. Often, the station plan includes 4*decorative* features such as murals showing local scenes, or a station may be used as a showcase for the work of important local artists. Building a new subway system may require the 4*destruction* of buildings on the surface, but attempts are made to cause as little 4*disruption* as possible. The 4*expansion* of an already-existing subway system can also be quite disruptive. Everyone looks forward to the day when the construction is over and the subway begins to 4*operate*. Often businesses spring up in and around a new subway station, contributing to the life of the neighborhood.

16*Unit: 4 ➜ Culture*
Origins of Writing (63-68), {p} 16*Excavators* working at ancient sites uncover objects that give us clues about life in the past. Household objects and tools that were used for 16*agriculture* can tell us a lot about how people lived long ago. Ancient people used a variety of materials to make the objects they used in daily life. Tools that were carved from wood, stone, and bone, and jars that were 16*created* from clay are some examples of objects that have been found. Some of these objects are quite beautiful, and it is clear that they were made by people with 16*specialized* skills. In ancient times, most people were not 16*literate*. However, traditional stories were an important part of ancient civilizations, and people told the 16*myths* of their culture to their children and grandchildren.

16*Unit: 4 ➜ Culture*
Hula Dancing In Hawaiian Culture (70-75), {p} Hula is a 11*traditional* dance from Hawaii. Originally, it was performed as part of religious rituals. It was danced 11*energetically* to the 11*accompaniment* of drums and chants. Over time it has been 11*influenced* in different ways, and styles have changed. Modern hula is a more graceful dance performed to guitar and ukulele music. It is performed for tourists and also as part of 11*celebrations*. The wide interest in hula dancing is 11*beneficial* for Hawaii, because it helps attract tourists to the islands.

16*Unit: 4 ➜ Culture*
The Art of Mime (77-82), {p} Everyone enjoys watching mimes perform. A really good mime makes the job look easy, but in reality it is impossible to 2*exaggerate* the skill required to perform mime 2*effectively*. It takes a 2*considerable* amount of time to develop techniques and learn to perform them well. Mimes need to be able to 2*portray* *characters* who will interest their audience, as well as perform a variety of illusions. The best mimes can show us our 2*frailties* while making us laugh at ourselves at the same time. Although mime has changed over the years, people still 2*reminisce* *about* some of the popular mimes of the past, who have inspired today's performers.

16*Unit: 5 ➜ Health*
Aerobic Exercise and Brain Health (90-94), {p} If you are suffering from a bad 4*mood* that won't go away, it is important to see a doctor. Mild depression may be a temporary response to the normal stresses of life, but ongoing depression could 4*indicate* a more serious condition. The doctor will ask you a series of questions and may recommend some tests to come up with a 4*diagnosis*. If your condition is 4*grave*, the doctor may give you medication. If, on the other hand, you are not suffering any serious disorder or 4*impairment*, the doctor may recommend something as simple as regular exercise.

column1:
16*Unit: 5 ➜ Health*
How Drugs Are Studied (96-100), {p} A good deal of time, effort, and money is required to thoroughly 10*investigate* a new drug before it can be put on the market. Scientists develop a 10*theory* about the ability of a certain substance to combat a specific disease or medical condition. Then they have to test their idea. After manufacturing the drug in the laboratory, they test it first on animals and then on people. They monitor the 10*absorption* of the drug by the body, and they look for any 10*toxins* that may be produced as the drug moves through the body. Then they test the drug's ability to combat the disease. If they get the outcome that they 10*desire* and the drug cures the disease or alleviates the condition, then it's time to work on marketing the product.

column1:
16*Unit: 6 ➜ Tourism*
Hiking the Inca Trail (102-107), {p} Hiking the Inca trail is a popular way to 15*access* Machu Picchu. However, the Peruvian government has 15*restricted* use of the trail, so not everyone is able to make the trip. The upside is that hiking is not the only way to get there, and because it is not a particularly 15*luxurious* way to travel, other methods might be preferable. You can get to the 15*archeological* site by train and bus, and they will get you there much faster than hiking. When you arrive in Cuzco, you can check the schedules. You may not be able to leave at 15*precisely* the time you wish, but you should be able to work out a schedule that is convenient. Whatever method you choose to get there, a trip to Machu Picchu is always a great 15*adventure* .

column1:
16*Unit: 6 ➜ Tourism*
What Is Ecotourism? (109-114), {p} Ecotourism companies operate on the principle that 16*avoidance* of harm to the environment is not only possible but an important part of pleasure trips. The basic 16*concept* of ecotourism is to respect the places you visit, in terms of both the culture and the natural environment. Ecotourism companies offer a wide range of tours. Some include stays in luxury hotels, whereas others take travelers to remote destinations where only the simplest 16*accommodations* are available. On some trips, travelers learn about the 16*wild* plants and animals that live in the area. On others, they learn about the local traditions. On all trips, travelers are careful not to cause any type of 16*injury* to the environment. Where environmental harm is concerned, they don't want to be among the culprits. If you are interested in ecotourism, it's easy to find out about trips being offered. Ecotourism companies 16*publicize* their trips in travel magazines and on travel websites.

column1:
16*Unit: 6 ➜ Tourism*
Learning Vacations (116-121), {p} Many people are interested in the concept of a learning vacation because it is a fun way to 3*acquire* skills while traveling. Learning vacations don't necessarily 3*cost* a great deal, so they are a good option for people on a budget. If you would like to travel to colorful parts of the world, taste exotic cuisine, and learn something new at the same time, then a learning vacation might be a good choice for you. If you want to 3*economize* , you can plan your vacation on your own. 3*Enrollment* in a short course on a topic of special interest is a common way to spend a learning vacation and an easy one to arrange. If the school that offers the course also has a 3*residence* for students, that will make your plans even easier. You don't need the 3*supervision* of a professional to organize your vacation. A little research online might provide you with all the information you need.❗2*Notes:*│ 8*A learning vacation,* often called 6*an "edu-vacation"* or study holiday, is *a trip that combines traditional leisure with structured skill development or educational exploration.* Unlike an intensive "boot camp," the primary goal is to maintain a holiday atmosphere while gaining hands-on experience in a unique location that directly informs the subject matter.
